__description: The xml module allows for parsing and generating XML data using Node objects.
__tinydesc: Parse and generate XML data with hierarchical Node objects

decode.__tinydesc: Parses an XML string and returns a Node object
decode.__description: Parses a string containing XML content and returns the root Node object representing the XML structure.

__params.__description: A string containing valid XML data to be parsed.

decode.__returns: Returns a Node object if parsing succeeds, or nil if the XML is invalid.
decode.__results: Node | nil
decode.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import xml

-- Parse XML string
local root = xml.decode("<root><greeting>Hello</greeting></root>")

-- Access child node
print(root[1].name) -- greeting

error.__tinydesc: Contains the last error message from XML operations
error.__description: A read-only string property containing the last error message from XML operation, or nil if no error occured.

load.__tinydesc: Load and parse an XML file into a Node object
load.__description: Loads and parses an XML file, returning the root Node object representing the XML structure.

__params.__description: A File object or a filename string representing the XML file to load.

load.__returns: Returns a Node object representing the root of the XML tree, or nil in case of error.
load.__results: Node nil
load.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import xml

-- Load an XML file
local root = xml.load("example.xml")

-- Print the root node name
print(root.name)

save.__tinydesc: Save a Node object to an XML file
save.__description: Serializes a Node object and writes it to an XML file.

__params.__description: A File object or a filename string representing the destination XML file.
The Node object to be saved.

save.__returns: Returns true on success or false in case of error.
save.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import xml

-- Create a new Node
local root = xml.Node("greeting")
root.text = "Hello, world!"

-- Save to file
xml.save("greeting.xml", root)

Node.__tinydesc: Represents an XML element with hierarchical structure
Node.__description: The Node object represents an XML element.

__constructor.__tinydesc: Creates a new Node object instance
__constructor.__description: Creates a new Node object instance to represent an XML element with child nodes, attributes and text content.

__params.__description: The name of the XML element , as a string
Optional text content of the XML element, as a string

__constructor.__returns: Returns a new Node object instance.
__constructor.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import xml

local root = xml.Node("greeting", "Hello")
print(root.text) -- Hello

add.__tinydesc: Add a child node to the current node
add.__description: Adds a new child node with the specified name and optional text content.

__params.__description: A string representing the name of the child node.
An optional string representing the content for the child node.

add.__returns: Returns the newly added Node object.
add.__example:
local xml = require 'xml'

local root = xml.Node("root")
local child = root:add("child", "content")
print(root) -- child have been added

text.__tinydesc: Get/set the text content of the node
text.__description: Gets or sets the text content of the node, as a string
text.__example:
local xml = require("xml")

local root = xml.encode("<book><title>WhiteSnow</title></book>")
local node = root[1] -- First node

print("Node name:", node.name)         --> "title"
print("Node text:", node.text)         --> "WhiteSnow"

name.__tinydesc: Get/set the name of the node
name.__description: Gets or sets the name of the node, as a string
name.__example:
local xml = require("xml")

local root = xml.decode("<book><title>WhiteSnow</title></book>")
local node = root[1] -- First node

print("Node name:", node.name)         --> "title"
print("Node text:", node.text)         --> "WhiteSnow"

parent.__description: Gets or sets the parent node of the current node. A nil value means the Node has no parent.
parent.__results: Node nil
parent.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import xml

-- Create a root node
local root = xml.Node("root")

-- Add a child node
local child = root:add("greeting", "Hello")

-- Access the parent of the child
print(child.parent.name)  -- Output: root

-- Reassign the parent (move child under a new node)
local newParent = xml.Node("newroot")
child.parent = newParent
print(child.parent.name)  -- Output: newroot

attributes.__description: A read-only table that allows dynamic access and modification of XML attributes using standard Lua table syntax.
attributes.__example:
local xml = require 'xml'

local root = xml.Node("user")
root.attributes.id = "123"
print(root.attributes.id) -- 123

Node length.__tinydesc: Gets the number of child nodes
Node length.__description: Using the length operator # will return the number of child nodes contained in the current node.
Node length.__example:
local xml = require 'xml'
local root = xml.Node('root')
root:add('child1', 'Hello')
root:add('child2', 'World')
print(#root)

__tostring.__tinydesc: Gets the string representation of the node
__tostring.__description: Returns the XML string representation of the node and its children.

__iterate.__description: Node object is iterable through the Luart helper function each(), returning the next Node's child with each loop iteration.
__iterate.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import xml

xml = require("xml")

local root = Node("root")
root:add("child1", "Hello")
root:add("child2", "World")
for child in each(root) do
    print(child.name, child.content)
end

remove.__tinydesc: Remove the current node from its parent
remove.__description: Detaches the current Node from its parent node. If the node has no parent, the operation does nothing.
remove.__returns: Returns true if the node was removed, or false if it had no parent.
remove.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import xml

xml = require("xml")

local root = xml.Node("root")
local title = root:add("title", "Hello")
print(#root)              -- 1
print(title:remove())     -- true
print(#root)              -- 0

clone.__tinydesc: Create a deep copy of the node to a new one.
clone.__description: Creates a deep copy of the current node, including its attributes and all descendant nodes.
clone.__returns: Returns a new Node identical to the original.
clone.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import xml

local book = xml.Node("book")
book:add("title", "Lua")
local copy = book:clone()
copy.name = "bookCopy"
print(copy[1].name)  -- title

query.__tinydesc: Find a node using a Path expression
query.__description: Executes a path expression (XPath 1.0 and XSLT 1.0 standards) relative to this node and returns the results as a table of Node, or nil if no match is found.
query.__returns: Returns the first matching Node, or nil if no match is found.
query.__example:
local xml = require("xml")

local root = xml.encode([[
<library>
  <book id="1"><title>WhiteSnow</title><author>Grimm brothers</author></book>
  <book id="2"><title>Cinderella</title><author>Charles Perrault</author></book>
</library>
]])

local function query_and_print(xpath)
    local nodes = root:query(xpath)
    print('XPath query : "'..xpath..'"')
    if nodes == nil then
        print("\tNo node found")
    else
        for node in each(nodes) do
            print("", node)
        end
    end
end

-- should print 2 nodes <book>
query_and_print("//book")

--- selects all <title> nodes inside <book>
--> should print 2 nodes (WhiteSnow and Cinderella)
query_and_print("//book/title")

--- selects all <author> nodes
--> should print 2 nodes(Grimm brothers and Charles Perrault)
query_and_print("//author")

--- selects all <book> nodes with attribute id="1"
--> should print 1 node (the first book)
query_and_print("//book[@id='1']")

--- selects <title> with the title text "Cinderella"
--> should print 1 node (the second book)
query_and_print("//book/title[text()='Cinderella']")

--- selects <book> whose author is "Grimm brothers"
--> should print 1 node (the second book)
query_and_print("//book[author='Grimm brothers']")

isleaf.__tinydesc: Checks if the node has no children
isleaf.__description: A read-only boolean property indicating whether this node has no child nodes.
isleaf.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import xml

xml = require("xml")

local n = xml.Node("item")
print(n.isleaf)  -- true
n:add("child")
print(n.isleaf)  -- false

Node[].__tinydesc: Get a child node by its index
Node[].__description: Allows to get a child node using table indexation using a number (the position of the node inside its parent). Returns the matching child Node or nil.
Node[].__example:
--! luart-extensions
import xml

xml = require("xml")

local root = xml.Node("root")
root:add("title", "The Wonderful Wizard of Oz")
root:add("author", "Lyman Frank Baum")
print(root[1].content)         -- 'The Wonderful Wizard of Oz'
Node[].__results: Node nil
